content_en:
“Between every two Athans'") there is a prayer.”'?!

'" The adhan and the Iqgamah. '2] Reported by Al-Bukhari in the Book of the adhan, in the Chapter: Between Every Two Athans There is a Prayer (627); and by Muslim in

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 518 prey! lst wgkd

Q. 287. Should the regular Sunnah prayers be made up for if the time for them has expired?

A. Yes, the regular Sunnah prayers may be made up for if the time for them has passed due to forgetfulness or sleep, because they are included in the genera] meaning of the words of the Prophet

8555 15) galls yt si ante ye 6 yn “Whoever slept through a prayer or forgot it, he must pray it when he remembers it."

Also, because of the Hadith of Umm Salamah, may Allah be pleased with her, in which it is stated that the Prophet # was distracted from performing two Rak‘ahs after the Zuhr prayer, so he made them up after the ‘Asr prayer.’?!

But if he abandoned it willfully until the time for it had expired, then he should not make it up for, because the regular Sunnah prayers are acts of worship which are to be performed at fixed times, and if a person deliberately offered them outside the prescribed time, they will not be accepted from him.

Q. 288. Has any evidence been reported for a person changing his place in order to perform the prayer after the obligatory prayer?

A. Yes, it has been reported in the Hadith of Mu‘awiyah, may

the Book of the Travellers’ Prayers, in the Chapter: Between Every Two Athans There is a Prayer (304).

"1 Reported by Al-Bukhari in the Book of the Times of the Prayers, in the Chapter: Whoever Forgot a Prayer, The He Must Pray it When he Remembers Hadith (597); and by Muslim in the Book of Masjids, in the Chapter: Making up for the Prayers Hadith 316.

'?1 Reported by Al-Bukhari in the Book of Forgetfulness, in the Chapter: If He Was Spoken to While He was Praying, He Would Point With His Hand and Listen (1176); and by Muslim in the Book of the Travellers’ Prayers, in the Chapter: The Two Rak‘ahs Which He Used to Pray (834).

Fatawa Relating to Prayer 519 Beall sgh

Allah be pleased with him, that he said: “The Prophet #% ordered us not to connect one prayer to another without speaking or going out.’!"!

The scholars have understood from this that it is desirable to separate the obligatory prayer from its Sunan by moving from the place in which the former was offered.

Q. 289. If the Sunnah of Ad-Duha"! is missed, can it be made up for or not?

A. If the time of Ad-Duha is missed, then the prayer is missed. This is because the Sunnah of Ad-Duha is restricted to this time. However the regular Sunnah prayers, because they follow on from the obligatory prayers, can be made up for, as can the Witr prayer. This is because it is confirmed in the Sunnah that: When the Prophet # was overcome by sleep or sickness, he would offer twelve Rak’ahs during the day.'*! So, Witr may also be made up for.

Q. 290. Is purification a condition for the acceptance of the prostration of recitation (Sajdatut-Tilawah)? And what is the correct thing to say in this prostration?

A. His Eminence the Shaikh answered by saying:

The prostration of recitation is a prostration legislated when a person recites a Qur’anic Verse in which there is a mention of prostration. The prostrations in the Qur’an are well known; and if a person wanted to prostrate, he should say: Allahu Akbar, then prostrate, and say:

"I Reported by Muslim in the Book of the Friday Prayer, in the Chapter: Prayer After the Friday Prayer (73).

'2] The voluntary prayer that is performed after the sun has risen.

'3] Reported by Muslim in the Book of the Travellers’ Prayers, in the Chapter: Combining the Night Prayers (139).

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 520 Pw Y! OISyT syle

“S35 dy ag ae 383 hj ale ill eo

(Subhana Rabbiyal A‘la, Subhanaka Allahumma Rabbana Wa Bihamdika, Allahummaghfirlee. Allahumma, Laka Sajadtu, Wa Bika Amantu, Wa Laka Aslamtu, Sajada Wajhiya Lil-lathee Khalaqahu, Wa Sawwarahu, Wa Shaqqa Sam‘ahu Wa Basarahu Bihawlihi Wa Quwwaltili.) “Glorified is my Lord, the Most High. Glory is to You, oh, Allah, my Lord, and praise is for You. Oh, Allah, forgive me. Oh, Allah! 1 have prostrated for You, and I have kelieved in You, and I have submitted myself to You. My face has prostrated to the One Who created it, and fashioned it, and brought forth its hearing and seeing by His Might and Power.’"'!

Bite Jukes he fe bey ete J oS vil 3513 Sale be GL SUS fo uly 35 (Allahummaktub Lee Biha Ajran, Wa Hutta ‘Anni Biha

Wizran, Waj’alha Lee ‘Indaka Thukhran, Wa Tagabbalha Minnee Kama Tagabbaltaha Min ‘Abdika Dawud.)

‘Oh, Allah, write for me by it a reward, and remove from me by if asin, and make it a treasure stored with You, and accept it from me as You accepted it from Your slave, Dawud.’'*!

Then he should raise his head without making Takbir and

''l Reported by Abu Dawud in the Book of Prayer, in the Chapter: What to Say if One Prostrates for Recitation (1414); and by At-Tirmithi in the Book of Prayer, in the Chapter: What to Say in the Prostration of the Qur’an Hadith (580) and he declared it to be authentic.

'2! Reported by Al-Bukhari in the Book of the adhan, in the Chapter: Completing the Tukbir in the Ruku’, Hadith (785); and by Muslim in the Book of Prayer, in the Chapter: Confirmation of the Takbir in Every,

Fatawa Relating to Prayer 521 Beal cold

without making Taslim, unless the Sajdah is during the prayer. When the reciter reads a Verse in which there is a Sajdah while he is praying, in that case, it is obligatory for him to make Takbir when he prostrates, and he must make Takbir when he stands up. This is because those who have described the prayer of the Prophet # have mentioned that he made Takbir whenever he lowered himself or raised himself up. And this includes the Sujud during the prayer and the Sujud of recitation.

As for what some of the people do, which is to make Takbir when they prostrate, and not to make Takbir when they stand up, and the Sujud is in the same prayer, I know of no basis for it in the Sunnah, nor in the sayings of the scholars.

As for the saying of the questioner: Is purification a condition for the acceptance of the prostration of recitation (Sajdatut-Tilawah)? this is a subject of dispute among the scholars: There are those among them who say that he must be in a state of ritual purification and there are those among them who say that it is not a condition and Ibn ‘Umar, may Allah be pleased with him, used to prostrate when he was not in a state of ritual purity.

But it is my considered opinion that it is more prudent not to prostrate except when one has performed ablution.

Q. 291. When should one perform the prostration of thanks to Allah (Sajdatush-Shukr)? And what is the manner ofit? And is it a condition that one make ablution?

A. The prostration of thankfulness is made when some catastrophe is averted or some blessing is given to a person. Like the prostration of recitation, it is outside prayer. Some of the scholars consider that it requires ablution and Takbir, while others hold that it requires the first Takbir only, then he should fall down in prostration and supplicate after his words: Subhana Rabbiyal-A‘la (Glorified is my Lord, the Most High).

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 522 pe ols,i ol

Q. 292. What is the ruling on Istikharah prayer? And should the supplication of Istikharah be said when a person offers the salutation to the Masjid or the regular Sunnah prayers?

A. Istikharah is a Sunnah when a person is concerned about something, and it is not clear to him whether it is better to do it or to leave it.

As for something about which it is clear to him whether doing it or leaving it is better, it is not prescribed to perform Istikharah for it, which is why the Prophet # would do many things, and he would not do them until after he had decided for sure to do them, and it has not been reported from him that he used to offer Istikharah prayer concerning them. So, if a man intended to pray, or pay Zakah, or to abandon unlawful deeds or the like, or he intended to eat or drink or sleep, it is not prescribed for him to offer Istikharah prayer.

The supplication of Istikharah should not be said when a person offers the prayer of salutation to the Masjid, or the regular Sunnah prayers, if he did not make the intention for it previously. This is because the Hadith clearly requires offering two Rak‘ahs for the performance of Istikharah, so if he prayed them without this intention, compliance would not have been achieved.

But if he made the intention to perform Istikharah when he offered the prayer of salutation to the Masjid, or the regular Sunnah prayers, then made the supplication of Istikharah, then it is clear from the Hadith that it will be counted for him, because he #% said:

aa Bi os we nd £555 3Slin ..then he should offer two Rak’ ‘ahs which are not from the obligatory prayers."1"1 "1 Reported by Al-Bukhari in the Book of Supplications, in the Chapter: The Supplication When Performing Istikharah (6382).

Fatawa Relating to Prayer 523 Ball gli

He did not make an exception for any prayer but the obligatory. It is possible that they will not be counted for him, because he said:

“If anyone of you intends to do something, then he should offer two Rak‘ahs.””

which proves that there is no reason for these two Rak‘ahs except Istikharah, so what I consider more fitting, is that he offer two independent Rak‘ahs, because this possibility exists, and it is possible that what is meant by singling out the obligatory prayers for exception is that he offers two voluntary Rak‘ahs, as if he had said: Let him offer two voluntary Rak‘ahs, and Allah knows best.

Q. 293. What is Tasbih prayer?

A. Tasbih prayer has not been authentically reported from the Prophet #; Imam Ahmad, may Allah have mercy on him, said: Regarding the Hadith about it, it is not authentic. ShaikhulIslam Ibn Taymiyyah, may Allah have mercy on him, said: It is a lie, and Ahmad and the Imams of his Mathhab have said that it is hated, and not a single Imam said that it was recommended. As for Abu Hanifah, Malik and Ash-Shafi‘i, none of them had heard of it.
content_id:
"Di antara setiap dua adzan terdapat salah (sunnah)."

Yaitu adzan dan iqamah. Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dalam Kitab Adzan, pada Bab: Di Antara Setiap Dua Adzan Terdapat Salah (627); dan oleh Muslim dalam

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 518 prey! lst wgkd

T. 287. Apakah salah sunnah rawatib harus diqadha jika waktunya telah berlalu?

J. Ya, salah sunnah rawatib boleh diqadha jika waktunya telah berlalu karena lupa atau tertidur, sebab hal itu termasuk dalam keumuman makna sabda Nabi ﷺ:

8555 15) galls yt si ante ye 6 yn "Barangsiapa yang tertidur dari suatu salah atau melupakannya, hendaklah ia menunaikannya ketika ia mengingatnya."

Juga karena hadith Ummu Salamah radhiyallahu 'anha, yang menyebutkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ pernah terhalang dari menunaikan dua rakaat setelah salah Zuhur, maka beliau mengqadhanya setelah salah Ashar.

Namun jika ia meninggalkannya dengan sengaja hingga waktunya berlalu, maka ia tidak perlu mengqadhanya, karena salah sunnah rawatib adalah ibadah yang dilakukan pada waktu-waktu tertentu, dan jika seseorang dengan sengaja menunaikannya di luar waktu yang ditentukan, maka tidak akan diterima darinya.

T. 288. Apakah ada dalil yang diriwayatkan tentang seseorang berpindah tempat untuk menunaikan salah setelah salah wajib?

J. Ya, hal itu diriwayatkan dalam hadith Mu'awiyah radhiyallahu

Kitab Salah Musafir, pada Bab: Di Antara Setiap Dua Adzan Terdapat Salah (304).

Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dalam Kitab Waktu-Waktu Salah, pada Bab: Barangsiapa yang Lupa Suatu Salah, Hendaklah Ia Menunaikannya Ketika Ia Mengingatnya, Hadith (597); dan oleh Muslim dalam Kitab Masjid, pada Bab: Mengqadha Salah yang Terlewat, Hadith 316.

Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dalam Kitab Sujud Sahwi, pada Bab: Jika Ia Diajak Bicara Saat Salah, Ia Memberi Isyarat dengan Tangannya dan Mendengarkan (1176); dan oleh Muslim dalam Kitab Salah Musafir, pada Bab: Dua Rakaat yang Biasa Beliau Tunaikan (834).

Fatawa Relating to Prayer 519 Beall sgh

'anhu, bahwa ia berkata: "Nabi ﷺ memerintahkan kami untuk tidak menyambung satu salah dengan salah lainnya tanpa berbicara atau keluar terlebih dahulu."

Para ulama memahami dari hadith ini bahwa disunahkan untuk memisahkan salah wajib dari salah sunnah rawatibnya dengan berpindah dari tempat salah wajib ditunaikan.

T. 289. Jika salah sunnah Dhuha terlewat, apakah boleh diqadha atau tidak?

J. Jika waktu Dhuha telah berlalu, maka salah tersebut telah terlewat. Hal ini karena sunnah Dhuha terikat pada waktu tersebut. Akan tetapi salah sunnah rawatib, karena mengikuti salah wajib, boleh diqadha, begitu pula salah Witir. Ini karena telah terkonfirmasi dalam Sunnah bahwa: Ketika Nabi ﷺ dikalahkan oleh rasa kantuk atau sakit, beliau menunaikan dua belas rakaat di siang hari. Maka, Witir pun boleh diqadha.

T. 290. Apakah bersuci merupakan syarat diterimanya sujud tilawah (Sajdatut-Tilawah)? Dan apa bacaan yang benar dalam sujud ini?

J. Yang Mulia Syaikh menjawab dengan mengatakan:

Sujud tilawah adalah sujud yang disyariatkan ketika seseorang membaca ayat Al-Quran yang di dalamnya terdapat penyebutan sujud. Ayat-ayat sujud dalam Al-Quran sudah diketahui; dan jika seseorang ingin sujud, ia hendaknya mengucapkan: Allahu Akbar, lalu sujud, dan membaca:

Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim dalam Kitab Salah Jumat, pada Bab: Salah Setelah Salah Jumat (73).

Yaitu salah sunnah yang ditunaikan setelah matahari terbit.

Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim dalam Kitab Salah Musafir, pada Bab: Menggabungkan Salah Malam (139).

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 520 Pw Y! OISyT syle

"S35 dy ag ae 383 hj ale ill eo

(Subhana Rabbiyal A'la, Subhanaka Allahumma Rabbana Wa Bihamdika, Allahummaghfirlee. Allahumma, Laka Sajadtu, Wa Bika Amantu, Wa Laka Aslamtu, Sajada Wajhiya Lil-lathee Khalaqahu, Wa Sawwarahu, Wa Shaqqa Sam'ahu Wa Basarahu Bihawlihi Wa Quwwaltili.) "Mahasuci Tuhanku Yang Maha Tinggi. Mahasuci Engkau, ya Allah, Tuhanku, dan segala puji bagi-Mu. Ya Allah, ampunilah aku. Ya Allah! Aku telah bersujud kepada-Mu, dan aku telah beriman kepada-Mu, dan aku telah berserah diri kepada-Mu. Wajahku bersujud kepada Dzat yang telah menciptakannya, dan membentuknya, dan membukakan pendengaran serta penglihatannya dengan daya dan kekuatan-Nya."

Bite Jukes he fe bey ete J oS vil 3513 Sale be GL SUS fo uly 35 (Allahummaktub Lee Biha Ajran, Wa Hutta 'Anni Biha

Wizran, Waj'alha Lee 'Indaka Thukhran, Wa Tagabbalha Minnee Kama Tagabbaltaha Min 'Abdika Dawud.)

'Ya Allah, catatkanlah untukku dengannya suatu pahala, dan hapuskanlah dariku dengannya suatu dosa, dan jadikanlah ia simpanan untukku di sisi-Mu, dan terimalah ia dariku sebagaimana Engkau menerimanya dari hamba-Mu, Dawud.'

Kemudian ia mengangkat kepalanya tanpa bertakbir dan

Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Dawud dalam Kitab Salah, pada Bab: Apa yang Dibaca Ketika Sujud Tilawah (1414); dan oleh At-Tirmidzi dalam Kitab Salah, pada Bab: Apa yang Dibaca dalam Sujud Al-Quran, Hadith (580) dan beliau menyatakannya sahih.

Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dalam Kitab Adzan, pada Bab: Menyempurnakan Takbir dalam Ruku', Hadith (785); dan oleh Muslim dalam Kitab Salah, pada Bab: Penetapan Takbir pada Setiap

Fatawa Relating to Prayer 521 Beal cold

tanpa mengucapkan salam, kecuali jika sujud tersebut dilakukan dalam salah. Ketika pembaca membaca ayat yang di dalamnya terdapat sujud saat ia sedang salah, dalam hal ini wajib baginya untuk bertakbir ketika sujud, dan ia harus bertakbir ketika berdiri. Ini karena mereka yang mendeskripsikan salah Nabi ﷺ menyebutkan bahwa beliau bertakbir setiap kali turun maupun bangkit. Dan ini mencakup sujud dalam salah dan sujud tilawah.

Adapun yang dilakukan sebagian orang, yaitu bertakbir saat sujud tetapi tidak bertakbir saat berdiri, padahal sujud tersebut dalam salah yang sama, saya tidak mengetahui dasarnya dalam Sunnah maupun dalam perkataan para ulama.

Adapun pertanyaan penanya: Apakah bersuci merupakan syarat diterimanya sujud tilawah (Sajdatut-Tilawah)? ini merupakan masalah yang diperdebatkan di kalangan ulama: Di antara mereka ada yang mengatakan bahwa ia harus dalam keadaan bersuci, dan di antara mereka ada yang mengatakan bahwa itu bukan syarat, dan Ibnu Umar radhiyallahu 'anhuma biasa sujud meskipun ia tidak dalam keadaan suci.

Namun menurut pendapat saya yang lebih hati-hati, hendaknya tidak sujud kecuali setelah berwudhu.

T. 291. Kapan seseorang melakukan sujud syukur kepada Allah (Sajdatush-Shukr)? Bagaimana tata caranya? Dan apakah disyaratkan berwudhu?

J. Sujud syukur dilakukan ketika suatu musibah dihindarkan atau suatu nikmat diberikan kepada seseorang. Seperti sujud tilawah, ia dilakukan di luar salah. Sebagian ulama berpendapat bahwa sujud syukur memerlukan wudhu dan takbir, sementara yang lain berpendapat bahwa hanya memerlukan takbir pertama saja, kemudian ia sujud dan berdu'a setelah mengucapkan: Subhana Rabbiyal-A'la (Mahasuci Tuhanku Yang Maha Tinggi).

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 522 pe ols,i ol

T. 292. Apa hukum salah istikharah? Dan apakah du'a istikharah dibaca ketika seseorang menunaikan salah tahiyyatul masjid atau salah sunnah rawatib?

J. Istikharah adalah sunnah ketika seseorang menghadapi suatu perkara, dan tidak jelas baginya apakah lebih baik melakukannya atau meninggalkannya.

Adapun perkara yang sudah jelas baginya apakah melakukan atau meninggalkannya lebih baik, maka tidak disyariatkan untuk melakukan istikharah untuknya. Itulah sebabnya Nabi ﷺ melakukan banyak hal, dan beliau tidak melakukannya kecuali setelah beliau memutuskan dengan pasti untuk melakukannya, dan tidak diriwayatkan dari beliau bahwa beliau menunaikan salah istikharah untuk hal-hal tersebut. Maka, jika seseorang bermaksud untuk salah, atau membayar zakah, atau meninggalkan perbuatan haram dan semacamnya, atau ia bermaksud untuk makan atau minum atau tidur, maka tidak disyariatkan baginya untuk menunaikan salah istikharah.

Du'a istikharah tidak boleh dibaca ketika seseorang menunaikan salah tahiyyatul masjid atau salah sunnah rawatib, jika ia tidak berniat untuk istikharah sebelumnya. Ini karena hadith tersebut secara jelas mensyaratkan menunaikan dua rakaat untuk istikharah, maka jika ia menunaikannya tanpa niat tersebut, maka belum terpenuhi ketentuannya.

Namun jika ia berniat melakukan Istikharah ketika ia menunaikan salat tahiyyatul masjid, atau salat Sunnah rawatib, kemudian membaca du'a Istikharah, maka jelas dari hadith bahwa hal itu akan dihitung baginya, karena beliau ﷺ bersabda:

"...maka hendaklah ia salat dua rakaat yang bukan dari salat wajib."(1) (1) Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dalam Kitab Du'a, Bab Du'a Ketika Melakukan Istikharah (6382).

Fatwa-fatwa Berkaitan dengan Salat

Beliau tidak mengecualikan salat apapun kecuali salat wajib. Ada kemungkinan bahwa hal itu tidak dihitung baginya, karena beliau bersabda:

"Jika salah seorang dari kalian berniat melakukan sesuatu, maka hendaklah ia salat dua rakaat."

Yang menunjukkan bahwa tidak ada alasan bagi dua rakaat ini kecuali untuk Istikharah. Maka yang saya anggap lebih tepat adalah hendaknya ia menunaikan dua rakaat yang berdiri sendiri, karena kemungkinan ini ada, dan mungkin yang dimaksud dengan pengecualian salat wajib secara khusus adalah agar ia menunaikan dua rakaat salat sunnah, seolah-olah beliau berkata: Hendaklah ia salat dua rakaat sunnah, wallahu a'lam.

Q. 293. Apakah salat Tasbih itu?

A. Salat Tasbih tidak diriwayatkan secara sahih dari Nabi ﷺ; Imam Ahmad, semoga Allah merahmatinya, berkata: Mengenai hadith tentangnya, ia tidak sahih. Syaikhul Islam Ibn Taymiyyah, semoga Allah merahmatinya, berkata: Itu adalah dusta, dan Ahmad serta para imam mazhabnya telah mengatakan bahwa hal itu makruh, dan tidak seorang imam pun yang mengatakan bahwa hal itu dianjurkan. Adapun Abu Hanifah, Malik dan Asy-Syafi'i, tidak seorang pun dari mereka yang pernah mendengarnya.

Should the regular السنة prayers be made up for if the time for them has expired? A. Yes, the regular السنة prayers may be made up for if the time for them has passed due to forgetfulness or sleep, because they are included in the genera] meaning of the words of النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم 8555 15) galls yt si ante ye 6 yn “Whoever slept through a الصلاة or forgot it, he must pray it when he remembers it." Also, because of الحديث النبوي الشريف of Umm Salamah, رضي الله عنها, in which it is stated that النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم # was distracted from performing two Rak‘ahs after the Zuhr الصلاة, so he made them up after the ‘Asr الصلاة.’?! But if he abandoned it willfully until the time for it had expired, then he should not make it up for, because the regular السنة prayers are acts of worship which are to be performed at fixed times, and if a person deliberately offered them outside the prescribed time, they will not be accepted from him. Q. 288. Has any evidence been reported for a person changing his place in order to perform the الصلاة after the واجب الصلاة? A. Yes, it has been reported in الحديث النبوي الشريف of Mu‘awiyah, may the Book of the Travellers’ Prayers, in the Chapter: Between Every Two Athans There is a الصلاة (304). The العلماء have understood from this that it is مستحب to separate the واجب الصلاة from its Sunan by moving from the place in which the former was offered. Q. 289. If السنة النبوية of Ad-Duha"! is missed, can it be made up for or not? A. If the time of Ad-Duha is missed, then the الصلاة is missed. This is because السنة النبوية of Ad-Duha is restricted to this time. However the regular السنة prayers, because they follow on from the واجب prayers, can be made up for, as can the Witr الصلاة. This is because it is confirmed in السنة النبوية that: When النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم # was overcome by sleep or sickness, he would offer twelve Rak’ahs during the day.'*! So, Witr may also be made up for. Q. 290. Is purification a condition for the acceptance of the prostration of recitation (Sajdatut-Tilawah)? And what is the correct thing to say in this prostration? A. His Eminence the Shaikh answered by saying: The prostration of recitation is a prostration legislated when a person recites a Qur’anic Verse in which there is a mention of prostration. The prostrations in the Qur’an are well known; and if a person wanted to prostrate, he should say: Allahu Akbar, then prostrate, and say: "I Reported by المسلم in the Book of the Friday الصلاة, in the Chapter: الصلاة After the Friday الصلاة (73). Then he should raise his head without making Takbir and ''l Reported by أبو داود in the Book of الصلاة, in the Chapter: What to Say if One Prostrates for Recitation (1414); and by At-Tirmithi in the Book of الصلاة, in the Chapter: What to Say in the Prostration of the Qur’an الحديث (580) and he declared it to be صحيح. '2! Reported by البخاري in the Book of the adhan, in the Chapter: Completing the Tukbir in the Ruku’, الحديث (785); and by المسلم in the Book of الصلاة, in the Chapter: Confirmation of the Takbir in Every, الفتاوى Relating to الصلاة 521 Beal cold without making Taslim, unless the Sajdah is during the الصلاة. When the reciter reads a Verse in which there is a Sajdah while he is praying, in that case, it is واجب for him to make Takbir when he prostrates, and he must make Takbir when he stands up. This is because those who have described the الصلاة of النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم # have mentioned that he made Takbir whenever he lowered himself or raised himself up. And this includes the Sujud during the الصلاة and the Sujud of recitation. As for what some of the people do, which is to make Takbir when they prostrate, and not to make Takbir when they stand up, and the Sujud is in the same الصلاة, I know of no basis for it in السنة النبوية, nor in the sayings of the العلماء. As for the saying of the questioner: Is purification a condition for the acceptance of the prostration of recitation (Sajdatut-Tilawah)? this is a subject of dispute among the العلماء: There are those among them who say that he must be in a state of ritual purification and there are those among them who say that it is not a condition and Ibn ‘Umar, رضي الله عنه, used to prostrate when he was not in a state of ritual purity. But it is my considered opinion that it is more prudent not to prostrate except when one has performed ablution. Q. 291. When should one perform the prostration of thanks to الله (Sajdatush-الشكر)? And what is the manner ofit? And is it a condition that one make ablution? A. The prostration of thankfulness is made when some catastrophe is averted or some blessing is given to a person. Like the prostration of recitation, it is outside الصلاة. Some of the العلماء consider that it requires ablution and Takbir, while others hold that it requires the first Takbir only, then he should fall down in prostration and supplicate after his words: Subhana Rabbiyal-A‘la (Glorified is my الرب, the Most High). الفتاوى Arkanul-الإسلام 522 pe ols,i ol Q. 292. What is the ruling on Istikharah الصلاة? And should the الدعاء of Istikharah be said when a person offers the salutation to the المسجد or the regular السنة prayers? A. Istikharah is a السنة when a person is concerned about something, and it is not clear to him whether it is better to do it or to leave it. As for something about which it is clear to him whether doing it or leaving it is better, it is not prescribed to perform Istikharah for it, which is why النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم # would do many things, and he would not do them until after he had decided for sure to do them, and it has not been reported from him that he used to offer Istikharah الصلاة concerning them. So, if a man intended to pray, or pay Zakah, or to abandon unlawful deeds or the like, or he intended to eat or drink or sleep, it is not prescribed for him to offer Istikharah الصلاة. The الدعاء of Istikharah should not be said when a person offers the الصلاة of salutation to the المسجد, or the regular السنة prayers, if he did not make the intention for it previously. This is because الحديث النبوي الشريف clearly requires offering two Rak‘ahs for the performance of Istikharah, so if he prayed them without this intention, compliance would not have been achieved. But if he made the intention to perform Istikharah when he offered the الصلاة of salutation to the المسجد, or the regular السنة prayers, then made the الدعاء of Istikharah, then it is clear from الحديث النبوي الشريف that it will be counted for him, because he #% said: aa Bi os we nd £555 3Slin ..then he should offer two Rak’ ‘ahs which are not from the واجب prayers."1"1 "1 Reported by البخاري in the Book of Supplications, in the Chapter: The الدعاء When Performing Istikharah (6382). الفتاوى Relating to الصلاة 523 Ball gli He did not make an exception for any الصلاة but the واجب. It is possible that they will not be counted for him, because he said: “If anyone of you intends to do something, then he should offer two Rak‘ahs.”” which proves that there is no reason for these two Rak‘ahs except Istikharah, so what I consider more fitting, is that he offer two independent Rak‘ahs, because this possibility exists, and it is possible that what is meant by singling out the واجب prayers for exception is that he offers two voluntary Rak‘ahs, as if he had said: Let him offer two voluntary Rak‘ahs, and الله knows best. Q. 293. What is Tasbih الصلاة? A. Tasbih الصلاة has not been authentically reported from النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #; الإمام Ahmad, رحمه الله, said: Regarding الحديث النبوي الشريف about it, it is not صحيح. ShaikhulIslam ابن تيمية, رحمه الله, said: It is a lie, and Ahmad and the الأئمة of his Mathhab have said that it is hated, and not a single الإمام said that it was مستحب. As for أبو حنيفة, Malik and Ash-Shafi‘i, none of them had heard of it.
